text.title: Prometheus Metrics and Health Probes
text.summary: RED method metrics, Prometheus middleware, SLO-aligned Histogram buckets, and liveness/readiness probe design.

rule.statement: Instrument every HTTP service with the RED method: Rate (requests/sec), Errors (4xx/5xx rate), Duration (latency Histogram). Define Histogram buckets that straddle your SLO thresholds.
rule.rationale: RED gives a complete picture of service health from the user's perspective. Buckets that don't straddle SLO thresholds cannot produce accurate quantile estimates at the SLO boundary.

rule.statement: Add metric labels only for bounded dimensions: method, endpoint (normalized path), status code. Never add request_id, user_id, or session as labels.
rule.rationale: High-cardinality labels cause Prometheus OOM. request_id belongs in logs, not metrics.

example.code:
from prometheus_client import Counter, Histogram, generate_latest from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware import time
REQUEST_COUNT = Counter( "http_requests_total", "Total HTTP requests", ["method", "endpoint", "status"] ) REQUEST_LATENCY = Histogram( "http_request_duration_seconds", "HTTP request latency", ["method", "endpoint"], buckets=[0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0] )
class MetricsMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware): async def dispatch(self, request, call_next): start = time.time() response = await call_next(request) REQUEST_COUNT.labels(request.method, request.url.path, response.status_code).inc() REQUEST_LATENCY.labels(request.method, request.url.path).observe(time.time() - start) return response

rule.statement: Liveness probe returns 200 if the process is alive (no DB checks). Readiness probe returns 200 only if all critical dependencies (DB, cache, critical external APIs) are healthy.
rule.rationale: A liveness probe that calls the DB triggers cascading restarts when the DB is slow, removing all instances. Keep liveness shallow; put dependency checks in readiness.

example.note: Use this path only for readiness; liveness should return immediately without any I/O.
